nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Frontmatter 'description: MSSA Mentor — teaches veterans software engineering by building real code alongside them. Use for: learning to code, building a first project, understanding a concept hands-on, MSSA bootcamp practice, transitioning from military to software engineering, mentor session, teach me X, help me build Y while explaining it.'
nodes.value: MSSA Mentor — teaches veterans software engineering by building real code alongside them. Use for: learning to code, building a first project, understanding a concept hands-on, MSSA bootcamp practice, transitioning from military to software engineering, mentor session, teach me X, help me build Y while explaining it.

nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Frontmatter 'description: Manage learner profiles and progress tracking. Use when: starting a session with a new or returning learner, detecting who the current learner is, tracking progress across sessions, adapting teaching style to individual preferences, coordinating multi-learner projects. Handles profile creation via interview, automatic validation via PowerShell scripts, progress persistence to Git, and teaching style adaptation.'
nodes.value: Manage learner profiles and progress tracking. Use when: starting a session with a new or returning learner, detecting who the current learner is, tracking progress across sessions, adapting teaching style to individual preferences, coordinating multi-learner projects. Handles profile creation via interview, automatic validation via PowerShell scripts, progress persistence to Git, and teaching style adaptation.

nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Frontmatter 'description: Run a ride-along session with an MSSA learner. Use when: starting a coding lesson, mentoring on a new concept, walking a veteran through their first project, learner asks 'help me build X and explain it', any non-trivial build that should also be a learning moment. Provides the full session shape: goal-setting, move-by-move build, naming concepts, after-action reviews.'
nodes.value: Run a ride-along session with an MSSA learner. Use when: starting a coding lesson, mentoring on a new concept, walking a veteran through their first project, learner asks 'help me build X and explain it', any non-trivial build that should also be a learning moment. Provides the full session shape: goal-setting, move-by-move build, naming concepts, after-action reviews.

nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Frontmatter 'description: Build progression that adds Azure Blob Storage attachment uploads to the TODO API from cad-todo-api-auth. Use when the learner wants to learn cloud storage SDKs, async I/O against a network service, multipart file uploads, content types, SAS tokens, and configuration secrets hands-on. Pairs with the ride-along method.'
nodes.value: Build progression that adds Azure Blob Storage attachment uploads to the TODO API from cad-todo-api-auth. Use when the learner wants to learn cloud storage SDKs, async I/O against a network service, multipart file uploads, content types, SAS tokens, and configuration secrets hands-on. Pairs with the ride-along method.

nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Frontmatter 'description: Build progression that deploys the secured TODO API (with blob attachments) from cad-blob-uploader to Azure App Service. Use when the learner wants to learn Azure App Service, deployment slots, app settings, Key Vault references, managed identity, and configuration-as-code hands-on. Pairs with the ride-along method.'
nodes.value: Build progression that deploys the secured TODO API (with blob attachments) from cad-blob-uploader to Azure App Service. Use when the learner wants to learn Azure App Service, deployment slots, app settings, Key Vault references, managed identity, and configuration-as-code hands-on. Pairs with the ride-along method.

nodes.label: When to break the method
nodes.description: Section 'When to break the method' in .github/skills/tracks/cloud-app-dev/cad-deploy-app-service/SKILL.md

nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Frontmatter 'description: Build progression that adds an Azure Function with a Storage Queue trigger to the TODO API ecosystem. When the API records a new attachment, it drops a message on a queue; the Function picks it up, reads the blob, computes a hash + size, and writes a metadata record back. Teaches serverless triggers, bindings, isolated worker model, idempotency, and poison queues. Use when the learner wants to learn Azure Functions, event-driven processing, queue triggers, blob input bindings, scale-to-zero, or serverless cost models. Pairs with the ride-along method.'
nodes.value: Build progression that adds an Azure Function with a Storage Queue trigger to the TODO API ecosystem. When the API records a new attachment, it drops a message on a queue; the Function picks it up, reads the blob, computes a hash + size, and writes a metadata record back. Teaches serverless triggers, bindings, isolated worker model, idempotency, and poison queues. Use when the learner wants to learn Azure Functions, event-driven processing, queue triggers, blob input bindings, scale-to-zero, or serverless cost models. Pairs with the ride-along method.

nodes.description: Frontmatter 'description: Build progression that swaps the in-memory store in cad-todo-api for a real database using Entity Framework Core and SQLite. Use when the learner wants to learn ORMs, DbContext, migrations, real async/await, or LINQ queries against a database hands-on. Pairs with the ride-along method.'
nodes.value: Build progression that swaps the in-memory store in cad-todo-api for a real database using Entity Framework Core and SQLite. Use when the learner wants to learn ORMs, DbContext, migrations, real async/await, or LINQ queries against a database hands-on. Pairs with the ride-along method.

nodes.value: Build progression for an ASP.NET Core Web API that exposes the TODO list from cad-todo-cli over HTTP. Use when the learner wants to learn web APIs, REST, controllers, dependency injection, or model binding hands-on. Pairs with the ride-along method.

notes: Markdown references that point at files not present in the repo appear as edges to missing nodes — merge.ps1 will detect these.
PS function call inference is best-effort by name match. False positives possible when names collide with parameters or strings.
Only first-level JSON fields are extracted to keep node count tractable; deepen by editing Extract-Json if needed.
